Zero rework. The stage-level review_1 verdict=ACCEPT binds to the stage diff_fingerprint 7fdbbf17...ce0e. Next: stage-level review-2.

Evidence: review-2-codex.raw-output.txt header + this field.
review_2.independent_test_rerun: node frontend/self-check.js PASS; PYTHONDONTWRITEBYTECODE=1 .venv/bin/python -m pytest -s -p no:cacheprovider backend/tests -q -> 39/39 passed (Codex bypassed read-only sandbox no-writebytecode); smoke rerun blocked by read-only sandbox loopback bind, consistent with committed 60-test-output.txt evidence.
review_2.note: Codex/GPT (gpt-5.5, xhigh, proxy2233) final-gate review = ACCEPT. Independently recomputed the stage diff_fingerprint (7fdbbf17...ce0e) and the three task fingerprints; all match status.json. Task boundaries clean. No forbidden interaction, Decimal discipline clean, same-origin frontend default confirmed, frozen inputs untouched, schema-valid snapshot with contract warnings preserved. review-2 evidence committed at c9f8788.

diff_fingerprint_note: Two scopes, one protocol. Task-level base/head/fingerprint recorded per task in status.json.tasks.<id> for task-level review-1 over each task's owner-scoped commit range. The stage-level top-level base/head/fingerprint (filled once all tasks are committed) covers the whole stage implementation diff and is the value review-2 and pre-accept bind to. status.json is excluded from the hash. Implementers may work in parallel, but commits are serialized by the controller so each task's base..head diff contains only that task's files.
commit_order_strategy: Controller commits serially to keep task fingerprints clean: H_intake (stage evidence, the task base) -> H_A (backend) -> H_B (frontend) -> H_C (integration evidence + status update). Kimi writes frontend/ and 20-implementation-frontend.md into the worktree only and does NOT git commit; the controller commits Task B. This guarantees Task A's base..head diff is backend-only and Task B's is frontend-only.

rationale: Implementation stage: the contract is frozen and the approved direction synthesis already covers Phase 1 public market discovery.
Multi-task (backend + frontend + integration), so a development breakdown was required and produced by Fable5 before coding.
No new human gate: public data only, 127.0.0.1 binding, no order/borrow/repay/transfer, no API keys.

findings: fixture == frozen contract-v2 6-symbol curated sample (6 rows); backend serves the full eligible TRADING perpetuals/TRADIFI market (688 rows). Summary totals differ by design; the contract check is the 6-symbol subset alignment (route_class/asset_tag/negative_funding_status), which matches exactly.

review_2_stage.runner_level_check: Decision-model pool for review-2 = {GPT/Codex, Anthropic}. Implementers claude_glm (zhipu) and kimi wrote delivery code -> hard-banned from review-2 (no override). Designer + breakdown_author = Fable5 (anthropic) -> prior design+breakdown involvement. direction_synthesis = codex (status=approved_prior_stage, covers this implementation stage; no new direction panel ran this stage, direction_panel_required=false). No decision model is provider-unrelated to ALL of {designer, direction_synthesizer, breakdown_author}: both decision vendors are involved (Codex=direction, Anthropic Fable5=design+breakdown) and both implementers are hard-banned. Per AGENTS.md ('review-2 prefers provider-level isolation... if no unrelated decision model is available after a runner-level check, Codex/GPT or Claude/Fable5 may review with a recorded design-involvement disclosure'), Codex reviews via the strong-reviewer disclosure override. Codex (prior direction_synthesis only) carries lighter involvement than Fable5 (design+breakdown), so Codex is selected. Codex CLI verified available at /Users/ark/.local/bin/codex. Evidence: this status.json.model_routing.review_2_stage + the disclosure section of review-2-codex.prompt.md + 50-review-2.md.
review_2_stage.reason: Review-2 final reviewer = Codex/GPT (gpt5.5, xhigh). Codex is the prior direction_synthesizer for the covering contract stage (user-approved, approved_prior_stage; no new direction panel this stage). Codex has NO design, breakdown, or code-authorship involvement this stage. Because no provider-unrelated decision model exists, Codex reviews via the strong-reviewer disclosure override (AGENTS.md 'GPT/Codex first' path) with reviewer_prior_involvement=direction_synthesis; the verdict must disclose the prior direction synthesis. Fallback (if Codex unavailable/invalid): Fable5 with design+breakdown disclosure, else decision_models_exhausted.

required_mitigation: Reviewers must recompute diff_fingerprint from base_sha and raw git diff, not controller summaries.
Test output must contain replayable raw command output.
Review prompts must pass raw artifact paths and raw diff paths, not controller paraphrases.
Task B review-1 must use a fresh read-only Claude-GLM session; the Task A controller transcript is not review evidence.

hard_constraints: No API key, signed endpoint, private account endpoint, user data stream.
No order, borrow, repay, transfer, websocket.
Backend writes only backend/** and its own report.
Frontend writes only frontend/** and its own report.
No planning calculator UI, manual-open ticket, order button, account status, or position management this stage.
The formal local page must load data from the same-origin /api/public-market/snapshot; fixture is for tests only.
funding_history default top-N=20; do not change without explicit user change.
Tests and CI are always offline; no network.

tests.detail: pytest 39 passed (offline frozen fixtures); float() audit clean; single-process http.server smoke OK; node frontend/self-check.js 10/10 PASS; integration Section 4 INTEGRATION OK. follow_up_coverage: Contract amended: snapshot.schema.json spot.match_type (nullable enum [exact_symbol, bstock_b_suffix_alias, null], not required); public-market-contract.md bStock note upgraded to frozen amendment (2026-07-03) with the resolve_spot_leg rule.
Backend resolve_spot_leg tries baseAsset+quoteAsset exact first, then (TRADIFI_PERPETUAL only) baseAsset+'B'+quoteAsset alias; spot.match_type exposed; CONTRACT_WARNINGS[2] updated.

classify.py UNCHANGED; the alias join yields MARGIN_SPOT_CANDIDATE (positive rate candidate) while negative_funding_status stays DISABLED_BSTOCK (cannot borrow) via the existing asset_tag priority — negative-funding execution remains disabled.
margin_public.source stays 'unverified'; no collateral ratio hard-coded (dynamic/unknown).
Backend classification/tests + frontend display repaired and independently reviewed (review-1 both ACCEPT, review-2 ACCEPT).
resolved_by_stage.note: Finding-status update only. impl-v1 review_1/review_2 verdicts and diff_fingerprint are NOT altered (status.json is excluded from impl-v1's hash; this is a finding-state transition, not a tampering with review conclusions). impl-v1 remains stage_accepted_waiting_user; both stages await user final acceptance together.

post_review_findings.summary: Binance now exposes bStocks spot/margin pairs with a B suffix (e.g. TSLABUSDT) while TRADIFI futures remain non-B symbols (e.g. TSLAUSDT). Current backend exact-symbol join leaves the 15 announced bStocks as PERP_ONLY_EXCLUDED with spot.symbol=null.
post_review_findings.impact: Review-2 remains valid for the frozen reviewed contract, but user final acceptance should wait until the public-market contract and implementation add a bStock alias rule and dynamic collateral fields.
required_follow_up: Amend contract: TRADIFI/BSTOCK spot leg lookup must try futures.baseAsset + 'B' + futures.quoteAsset before declaring PERP_ONLY.
Expose actual bStock spot symbol and match source, e.g. spot.match_type=bstock_b_suffix_alias.
Keep negative-funding execution disabled for bStocks because borrowing is not currently supported.
Represent collateral ratio as dynamic/unknown until a signed or page-backed collateral ratio source is integrated; do not hard-code a percentage.
Repair backend classification/tests and frontend display before final user acceptance.

next_action: Review-1 and review-2 accepted this stage against the frozen contract and validate-stage.py --phase pre-accept PASSED. The post-review P1 finding bstocks_b_suffix_spot_margin_alias is now resolved_by_stage 2026-07-public-market-bstock-alias-v1 (head 0842820): that stage amended the contract + repaired backend/frontend/tests and passed its own review-1 (both ACCEPT) + review-2 (Codex ACCEPT) + pre-accept. impl-v1's own review_1/review_2 verdicts and diff_fingerprint are unchanged. impl-v1 remains stage_accepted_waiting_user; both stages await user final acceptance together (controller does not declare accepted).
